title: Support for customizing hypervisor command line parameters
body:
### Feature request description

Request: I sugggest that `podman machine` provide some documented means of changing key hypervisor parameters.

Description of the problem this would help solve:

- Platform hardware: MacBook Pro M1 Max, 64 GB RAM, 2 TB SSD
- Platform OS: macOS Sonoma 14.7.6
- Virtualization platform: VirtualBox 7.1.10
- "Host" OS: Ubuntu desktop 25.04 (ARM64)
- Podman version: 5.4.1 (Ubuntu 25.04)
- QEMU version: 9.2.1 (Ubuntu 25.04)

I am trying to use podman running under a virtualized guest OS (Ubuntu 25.04) in a unique environment, for which I don't expect direct support.

KVM is not available in this configuration, but podman appears to require it when running in a Linux ARM64 environment. It appears some key command line parameters for `qemu` on Linux/aarch64 are hardcoded in `podman/pkg/machine/qemu/options_linux_arm64.go`. I don't see a way to override them short of rebuilding podman from source.

The `qemu` command line in this case is:

```
DEBU[0000] qemu cmd: [/usr/bin/qemu-system-aarch64 -accel kvm -cpu host -M virt,gic-version=max,memory-backend=mem -bios /usr/share/qemu-efi-aarch64/QEMU_EFI.fd -drive if=virtio,file=/home/cfry/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default-arm64.qcow2 -object memory-backend-memfd,id=mem,size=2048M,share=on -m 2048 -smp 2 -fw_cfg name=opt/com.coreos/config,file=/home/cfry/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.ign -qmp unix:/run/user/1000/podman/qmp_podman-machine-default.sock,server=on,wait=off -netdev stream,id=vlan,server=off,addr.type=unix,addr.path=/run/user/1000/podman/podman-machine-default-gvproxy.sock -device virtio-net-pci,netdev=vlan,mac=5a:94:ef:e4:0c:ee -device virtio-serial -chardev socket,path=/run/user/1000/podman/podman-machine-default.sock,server=on,wait=off,id=apodman-machine-default_ready -device virtserialport,chardev=apodman-machine-default_ready,name=org.fedoraproject.port.0 -pidfile /run/user/1000/podman/podman-machine-default_vm.pid -chardev socket,id=virtiofschar0,path=/run/user/1000/podman/virtiofschar0 -device vhost-user-fs-pci,queue-size=1024,chardev=virtiofschar0,tag=a394d4df67cd74b059e12d0d191ccf94d29e] 
```

The resulting error message:

```
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code 1, stderr: qemu-system-aarch64: -accel kvm: Could not access KVM kernel module: No such file or directory
qemu-system-aarch64: -accel kvm: failed to initialize kvm: No such file or directory
```

`-accel kvm` is the specific option that causes trouble in this configuration. I can't find a way to get Ubuntu 25.04 to provide KVM. (I don't believe it is possible with the Apple M1 CPU, which is known not to support nested virtualization.) And I can't find a way to modify the `qemu` command to use a different accelerator with this version of podman.

### Suggest potential solution

I would like to see podman provide documented support for modifying all hypervisor command line parameters. E.g. `podman machine init` could write the command line (or just its parameters) to a configuration file in the user's default configuration directory, where the user could tweak it as required. Or perhaps `podman machine init` could take additional optional command line parameters to override the default parameters for the platform.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

The only alternative I can see at present is modifying the podman source files and rebuilding from source. I can't change the hardware configuration, and I don't see a way to configure VirtualBox or Ubuntu to provide KVM given the hardware.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body:
Podman machine (using QEMU hypervisor) used to have the command line stored in machine configuration before. It lead to a number of usability problems and this was removed during one of the refactorings. I would not expect this one coming back in any form except additional settings available during `machine init` which will be translated into command line, but this effort has to be justified, because it would require new tests (sometimes with test env setup) and documentation efforts (because it is required for all user facing features).

Beside these technicalities. Running Podman in a VM w/o nested virtualization acceleration will be tricky at least. The performance will be underwhelming. It might even not run stable. And if this scenario would become considered as supported this would probably require at least some e2e tests in CI to cover basic regressions, which will make CI runs very long.

I agree, podman machine is not a generic virtual machine manager so I rather not expose more options.

@plexilcrf Why are you trying to use podman machine in the first place? Just run podman natively on linux, using a VM inside a VM seems like a bad idea

> [@plexilcrf](https://github.com/plexilcrf) Why are you trying to use podman machine in the first place? Just run podman natively on linux, using a VM inside a VM seems like a bad idea

I'm trying to reproduce a bug report in other software, where the reporting user is using Windows -> Ubuntu 25.04 under WSL. My desktop machine happens to be an M1 Mac. I don't have a Windows or Linux machine at my fingertips. I've moved my testing to a remote Linux machine.

> Beside these technicalities. Running Podman in a VM w/o nested virtualization acceleration will be tricky at least. The performance will be underwhelming. It might even not run stable. And if this scenario would become considered as supported this would probably require at least some e2e tests in CI to cover basic regressions, which will make CI runs very long.

As I said in my original report, I do not expect support for this configuration.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm closing this PR as we have no plans to implement this.
